source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Cloud Storage IAM Permission Changes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Cloud Storage IAM Permission Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Cloud Storage Bucket IAM changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring changes to cloud storage bucket permissions may reduce the time needed to detect and correct permissions on sensitive cloud storage buckets and objects inside the bucket. Impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. For each project that contains cloud storage buckets, go to Logging/Logs- based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure at least one metric <Log_Metric_Name> is present with the filter text: resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name> stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than 0 seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>. Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page appears. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notifications channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud beta logging metrics create Create the prescribed alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/logs-based-metrics/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/custom-metrics/ 3. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerts/ 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 5. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/overview 6. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam-roles
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be audited using GCP APIs, Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), and GCP CLI commands.
- The audit field provides explicit CLI commands for verifying the existence of log metrics and alerting policies.
- GCP services like Logging and Monitoring have APIs and CLI commands available to check the required configurations.
- The audit mentions specific Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands to list log metrics and alert policies, further confirming the feasibility of automating this control check.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_storage_bucket_iam_permission_alert_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to verify the presence of user-defined log metrics related to Cloud Storage IAM permission changes with the specific filter: `resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"`.
- Use `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` to ensure there is at least one alert policy that uses the log metric created above.
- Verify that the alert policy condition `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"` and that `enabled` is set to true.
- Check if alert policies are configured to alert when any new owner change occurs (i.e., when the metric condition is above a threshold of 0).
- Ensure that appropriate notification channels are configured for the alert policy.
- Pass if the log metric and alert policy are correctly configured according to the specifications; fail if either the metric or alert policy is missing or misconfigured.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated**: The classification as "Automated" is correct. The existence and configuration of log metrics and alerting policies in GCP can be checked programmatically using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) CLI.
- **GCP SDK/CLI Capability**: 
  - `gcloud logging metrics list` can enumerate user-defined log metrics and their filters.
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list` (and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list`) can enumerate alerting policies and their conditions.
- **Other GCP Tools**: While GCP Config (now called Config Controller) can model some resources, it does not natively cover log metrics and alerting policies as first-class resources for compliance checks. CloudFormation is AWS-specific and not relevant here.
- **Conclusion**: The control can be fully and reliably checked via GCP APIs and CLI, so the automated classification is justified.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

**Strengths:**
- The use of `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` is correct for enumerating log metrics.
- The filter string `resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"` is accurate for detecting IAM permission changes on GCS buckets.
- The use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` is technically valid, though the non-alpha version (`gcloud monitoring policies list`) is now generally available and preferred for production automation.
- The check for alert policy condition using `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"` is correct.
- The requirement to check for notification channels and enabled status is appropriate.

**Gaps / Issues:**
- The program name, while descriptive, could be more specific (see Improvements).
- The description does not specify how to programmatically verify that the alert policy is specifically tied to the correct log metric (i.e., parsing the alert policy JSON to ensure the metric filter matches the log metric created for GCS IAM changes).
- No mention of project scoping or cross-project checks (if required).
- No explicit mention of verifying that the alert threshold is set to trigger on any IAM permission change (i.e., threshold = 0).
- No mention of validating the notification channel type (e.g., email, SMS, webhook) or ensuring it is active.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Program Name**: 
  - Current: `gcp_logging_storage_bucket_iam_permission_alert_configured`
  - Suggested: `gcp_logging_gcs_iam_permission_change_alert_configured` or `gcp_logging_gcs_bucket_iam_change_alert_configured`
    - Rationale: Use "gcs" for Google Cloud Storage, "bucket" for resource type, and "iam_change" for specificity.
- **Command Modernization**: Use `gcloud monitoring policies list` instead of the alpha command, unless a specific alpha-only feature is needed.
- **Deeper Validation**: 
  - Ensure the log metric filter exactly matches the required pattern.
  - Parse alert policy JSON to confirm the metric is the one created for GCS IAM changes.
  - Confirm the alert threshold is set to trigger on any event (threshold = 0).
  - Validate that at least one notification channel is enabled and active.
- **Project/Scope**: Clarify if the check should be performed across all projects or only a specific project.
- **Alternative API Methods**: 
  - Directly use the [Logging Metrics API](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/v2/rest/v2/projects.metrics/list) and [Monitoring API](https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/ref_v3/rest/v3/projects.alertPolicies/list) for more granular automation.
- **Documentation**: Reference the relevant GCP documentation for metric and alert policy configuration.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is fundamentally correct and technically feasible with GCP APIs and CLI.
- Minor improvements are needed for completeness and robustness, but the core approach is sound.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Keep as Automated**, but:
  - Update the program name for clarity and convention.
  - Use the stable (non-alpha) `gcloud monitoring policies list` command.
  - Add explicit steps to parse and validate that the alert policy is directly tied to the correct log metric and that the threshold is set appropriately.
  - Ensure notification channels are both present and active.
  - Optionally, clarify project scoping and reference relevant GCP documentation.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section              | Result/Score | Notes                                                                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE        | Fully automatable via GCP APIs/CLI                                                    |
| Technical Accuracy   | 8/10         | Correct commands, but could be more robust and specific                               |
| Improvements         | -            | Update program name, use non-alpha CLI, deeper validation, clarify scope              |
| Confidence           | HIGH         | Approach is fundamentally correct                                                     |
| Final Recommendation | MODIFY       | Minor but important improvements for completeness and clarity                         |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the existence and configuration of log-based metrics and alerting policies for Cloud Storage IAM permission changes can be fully verified using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The required properties (metric filters and alert policy conditions) are directly accessible via CLI/API, making this control suitable for reliable automation without manual intervention.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_logging_gcs_bucket_iam_permission_change_alert_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:**  
  - Cloud Logging (Log-based Metrics)  
  - Cloud Monitoring (Alerting Policies)
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format=json`  
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list --format=json`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - For log-based metrics:  
    - Metric exists with filter: `resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"`
  - For alerting policies:  
    - Policy exists with a condition referencing the above log metric (metric type: `logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log_Metric_Name>`)
    - Condition threshold is set to trigger on metric value > 0
    - Alert policy is enabled (`enabled: true`)
    - Notification channel(s) are configured
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - At least one user-defined log metric with the exact filter exists  
  - At least one alerting policy is enabled and references this metric, with a threshold to alert on any permission change, and has a notification channel
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - No such log metric exists  
  - No alerting policy references the metric, or the policy is disabled, or lacks a notification channel

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements; both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The only minor difference was the use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` versus the stable `gcloud monitoring policies list`—the stable version is now preferred and used in this final decision.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
